concepts.title: Format of a Formal Notice
concepts.description: A formal notice typically includes a heading, date, reference number (if any), salutation, body, and a formal closing such as 'Yours faithfully' or 'Yours sincerely'.

concepts.title: Key Elements of a Notice
concepts.description: Key elements of a notice include a clear heading, specific details, a formal tone, and a specific call to action or instruction.

concepts.title: Tone and Format
concepts.description: The tone of a formal notice is objective and professional, while the format is structured and concise, making it easy to understand and act upon.

concepts.title: Language and Vocabulary
concepts.description: Formal notices use simple, clear, and concise language, avoiding jargon and technical terms, and are written in the third person.

concepts.title: HEADS
concepts.description: To remember the key elements of a notice, use the mnemonic HEADS: Heading, Date, Essential details, Audience, and Signature.

concepts.title: Using Informal Language
concepts.description: Avoid using informal language, slang, or colloquial expressions in formal notices, as they can undermine the authority and professionalism of the notice.

concepts.title: Types of Notices
concepts.description: Common types of notices include public notices, private notices, official notices, and advertisements, each with its own specific purpose and format.

concepts.title: Purpose of a Notice
concepts.description: The primary purpose of a notice is to convey information, provide instructions, or announce events, and it should be clear, concise, and easy to understand.
